hypothesis: Incorporating a differentiable tolerance-aware verifier that simulates manufacturing thickness perturbations (±Δt) and interfacial roughness as stochastic perturbations, and training CHROMA to minimize the expected color error ∆E over these perturbations, will yield multilayer stacks whose color remains within the perceptual JND (∆E ≤ 2) across a wider range of thickness variations and viewing angles than designs produced without variability modeling.
